title: 🎙️ Community Tech & Governance
description: Today we look at how Cariboo communities govern themselves and use technology for public good — from school district digital initiatives and rural broadband projects to council decisions and civic innovation. We're interested in stories where technology and local democracy intersect, and how small municipalities are solving big problems with limited resources. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we look at how Cariboo communities govern themselves and use technology for public good — from school district digital initiatives and rural broadband projects to council decisions and civic innovation. We're interested in stories where technology and local democracy intersect, and how small municipalities are solving big problems with limited resources.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Community Tech & Governance' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: municipal broadband and fiber projects in Cariboo communities, SD27 education technology and digital learning, civic tech platforms and open data initiatives, community-owned networks and ISPs, smart rural infrastructure (water, waste, transit), digital government services for rural areas, council decisions on technology spending, public Wi-Fi and connectivity programs, election technology and civic engagement platforms, rural innovation hubs, and how Williams Lake/Quesnel/100 Mile/CRD use technology for public services. Prefer articles that bridge governance AND technology in rural civic contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Study finds infrasound the likely horror in hauntings
items.content_html:
Noise below the range of human hearing from old pipes and mechanical systems can induce stressful sensations, according to a study published by Canadian researchers in Frontiers in Behavioral Neuroscience. The findings offer a compelling scientific explanation for why I feel ill-at-ease in the basement of the abandoned 19th-century sanitarium, built over an old cemetery, that I've been squatting in rural West Pennsylvania. — Read the rest

The post Study finds infrasound the likely horror in hauntings appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Tor Browser's "New Identity" button didn't actually reset your fingerprint
items.content_html:
Tor Browser has a "New Identity" button that's supposed to give you a completely fresh start — like opening a brand-new browser with no memory of what you were doing before. But it turns out it didn't fully do that.

Researchers discovered that a built-in browser feature (called IndexedDB, which websites use to store data on your device) could leave behind a kind of hidden "fingerprint." — Read the rest

The post Tor Browser's "New Identity" button didn't actually reset your fingerprint appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Scientists finally cracked how bacteria's spinning motor actually works
items.content_html:
Mike Manson has spent 50 years at Texas A&M studying the bacterial flagellar motor — a molecular machine that spins hundreds of times per second, outpacing a race car's spinning crankshaft, to drive bacteria through water. He finally understands it. "My lifelong quest is now fulfilled," he told Quanta Magazine. — Read the rest

The post Scientists finally cracked how bacteria's spinning motor actually works appeared first on Boing Boing.
